compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Completion Verification: PASS

All acceptance criteria are satisfied by the implementation. Evidence is grounded in tool output.

---

### AC 1 — Guard is enforced UNCONDITIONALLY (no --force / --force-close bypass)
**MET.** `transition.py:408–426`: the `raise CommandError(...)` fires whenever `target_status == "closed"` and `open_children` is non-empty. It is not conditioned on `force` or `force_close` — those variables are never tested before the raise. The bug's "WARNING but succeeds" path is gone.

### AC 2 — --force still bypasses plan-review; --force-close still bypasses signature/verifier
**MET.** `transition.py:363–371`: `force_reason = (reason or "(no reason given)") if force else ""` still feeds `gates.plan_review_precheck` with a non-empty reason when `force=True`, preserving the bypass. `transition.py:214–215`: `if force_close: return None` still short-circuits the completion-verification gate. Confirmed by `test_plan_review_gate.py:167–177` (`test_force_bypasses_gate_with_audit`).

### AC 3 — Error message directs to close/detach; does NOT offer --force as an escape
**MET.** `transition.py:420–424`: `"the child-closure invariant cannot be bypassed (not with --force or --force-close). Close or resolve these children first, or detach them (re-home), then close:"` — no mention of `--force` as an option.

### AC 4 — --force help text updated to drop the children-guard-skip claim
**MET.** `transition.py:47–49` (`_USAGE`): `"--force  Bypass the plan-review gate … Does NOT bypass the unresolved-children close guard (a structural invariant — close/detach children first)."` — the old claim is reversed and explicitly corrected.

### AC 5 — Tests assert force/force-close cannot close a parent over open children; old skip-tests removed
**MET.** `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_transition_open_children.py`:
- `test_force_does_NOT_close_parent_with_open_child` (lines 51–63): `--force` → `rc==1`, `"unresolved"` in output, `"cannot be bypassed"` in output, parent stays `open`.
- `test_force_close_does_NOT_close_parent_with_open_child` (lines 66–77): `--force-close=emergency` → `rc==1`, `"unresolved"` in output, parent stays `open`.
No old "force bypasses children guard" tests found anywhere in the suite.

### AC 6 — make lint + full suite pass
Out of scope for completion verification (CI/quality gate).

### Bug resolution
The defect no longer exists in the code: the guard at `transition.py:408–426` raises unconditionally on open children — `force` and `force_close` are not consulted before the error is thrown.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

`rebar transition <parent> closed --force` currently SKIPS the unresolved-children guard, letting a parent (epic/story) close while it has open (non-closed) children. The child-closure relationship is a STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY invariant (a parent is not complete if its children are not), NOT a quality gate — so --force should never be able to violate it. (Contrast: --force-close legitimately bypasses the signature/completion-verifier QUALITY gate for emergencies; --force legitimately bypasses the plan-review gate. Neither should bypass child-closure.)

Surfaced live: an epic was force-closed over 2 open children (then reopened + properly resolved by detaching the follow-ups). The footgun should not exist.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Create an epic with an open child (e.g. `rebar create epic E`, `rebar create task T --parent E`).
2. `rebar transition E in_progress closed --force` (or `--force --force-close=...`).
3. Observe: the epic closes with a WARNING but succeeds, leaving an open child under a closed parent.

## Expected vs Actual

EXPECTED: closing a ticket with any open (non-closed) child is ALWAYS refused, regardless of --force / --force-close. The only ways to close are: close/resolve the children first, or detach them (re-home). The error should NOT offer --force as an escape for the children guard.

ACTUAL: --force skips the unresolved-children guard (transition.py:~47-48 help + ~416-425 logic + the force branch at ~119), closing the parent with children unresolved (only a Warning).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The unresolved-children close guard is enforced UNCONDITIONALLY: --force and --force-close do NOT bypass it; closing a ticket with >=1 open child always errors.
- [ ] --force still bypasses the plan-review gate; --force-close still bypasses the signature/completion-verifier requirement (those are unchanged).
- [ ] The error message no longer suggests --force as a way to close over open children; it directs to close/resolve or detach the children.
- [ ] The --force help text is updated to drop the children-guard-skip claim.
- [ ] A test asserts a parent with an open child cannot be closed even with --force/--force-close; tests covering the old force-skip behavior are updated/removed.
- [ ] make lint + full suite pass.

file_impact.reason: make the open-children close guard unconditional (force no longer bypasses it) + update help
invert the force-over-children test; add force-close rejection test

compiled_state.title: --force must NOT bypass the unresolved-children close guard (structural invariant, not a quality gate)
